questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The highest common factor (HCF) of $24$ and $36$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** HCF is the product of common prime factors at lowest powers.

**Working.** $24=2^3\cdot 3$, $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$; HCF $=2^2\cdot 3=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: MINER : ORE :: FARMER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'worker : product extracted/produced'.

**Working.** A miner extracts ore; a farmer produces a crop. A tractor and field are tools/locations, not products.

**Answer.** crop.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central angle $=\text{fraction}\times360^\circ$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=0.25\times360=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The perimeter of a square of side $9$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perimeter of a square $=4\times\text{side}$.

**Working.** $4\times 9 = 36$.

**Answer.** $36$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Some books are pens. All pens are red. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine the particular and universal premises.

**Working.** The books that are pens must be red (all pens are red), so some books are red.

**Answer.** Some books are red.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many squares (of all sizes) are there in the $3\times3$ grid shown?

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 grid of unit squares.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum of squares of sizes $1,2,3$: $3^2+2^2+1^2$.

**Working.** $9+4+1=14$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a square split into four quadrants, the shaded quadrant moves clockwise each step: top-left, top-right, bottom-right, ... The next shaded quadrant is

figure.caption: Three squares with the shaded quadrant at top-left, top-right and bottom-right respectively.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'><rect x='15' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='95' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='175' y='20' width='40' height='40'/></g><rect x='15' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='115' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='195' y='40' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continue the clockwise motion of the shaded quadrant.

**Working.** Top-left, top-right, bottom-right, then bottom-left.

**Answer.** Bottom-left.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A water image is a reflection about a horizontal line. Which digit looks the same as its water image?

figure.caption: The digit 8 with a dashed horizontal reflection line through its middle.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='50' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>8</text><line x1='15' y1='60' x2='125' y2='60' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A digit unchanged by a horizontal flip has a horizontal axis of symmetry.

**Working.** '8' is symmetric top-to-bottom, so its water image is identical; 2, 5 and 7 are not.

**Answer.** 8.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $5, 11, 23, 47, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term follows $a_{n+1}=2a_n+1$.

**Working.** $47\times 2 + 1 = 95$.

**Answer.** $95$.

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $2000\,\text{kg}$ at $40\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$2000(1-40/100)=1200.$$

**Answer.** $1200\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 300 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{300\,\text{s}}\times1000=5\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $5\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Compared with a slow sand filter, a rapid sand filter is characterised by
options: higher filtration rate and reliance on prior coagulation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rapid sand filters run at ~100–125 times the slow-filter rate, need coagulation–sedimentation upstream, and are cleaned by backwashing.

**Working.**
Higher rate, needs coagulation.

**Answer.** High rate, coagulation-dependent

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Which are recognised biodiversity hotspots in/around India?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Western Ghats, Himalaya and Indo-Burma are global hotspots; the Thar is not (low endemism/richness threshold).

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Western Ghats, Himalaya, Indo-Burma

questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $4$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 8000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$8000\times0.1^{3}=8.$$

**Answer.** $8$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: First-order decay, $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** t½ = ln2/k.

**Working.**
$$0.693/0.231=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.solution:

**Working.**
Climax community.

**Answer.** Climax community

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A treatment plant receives BOD $200\,\text{mg/L}$ and discharges $30\,\text{mg/L}$. The BOD removal efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 200</text><rect x="190" y="132" width="46" height="18" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="128" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 30</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">BOD (mg/L)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta=\dfrac{S_0-S_e}{S_0}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{200-30}{200}\times100=85\%.$$

**Answer.** $85\%$

**Answer.** $80\,\text{kcal/m}^2/\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A direct consequence of eutrophication in a water body is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Algal blooms die and decompose, consuming DO and raising BOD.

**Working.**
DO depletion.

**Answer.** Oxygen depletion

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A hyperaccumulator crop yields $20\,\text{t/ha}$ of dry biomass containing $500\,\text{mg/kg}$ of nickel. The nickel phytoextracted per hectare in one harvest is _____ g/ha.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal extracted $=$ dry biomass $\times$ tissue concentration. With biomass $20\,\text{t/ha}=20{,}000\,\text{kg/ha}$ and $500\,\text{mg/kg}$: $(20{,}000)(500)/1000$ g.

**Working.**
$$20{,}000\times500/1000=10{,}000\,\text{g/ha}.$$

**Answer.** $10\,000\,\text{g/ha}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $6\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 21</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$6\times21=126.$$

**Answer.** $126$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: For the landfill shown, read the usable air-space and the annual fill rate from the diagram. Its design life is _____ years.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life = air-space/annual fill, both read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{300000}{40000}=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\text{yr}$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$50+5\times28+0.5\times265=322.5.$$

**Answer.** $322.5$

questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="142.5" width="46" height="7.5" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="138.5" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">5</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">1</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: An enzyme follows Michaelis–Menten kinetics. Reading $V_{max}$ (plateau) and $K_m$ (half-saturation) from the curve shown, the reaction velocity at substrate concentration $[S]=6\,\text{mmol/L}$ is _____ µmol·L⁻¹·min⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">V_max = 8</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">V_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_m = 2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Michaelis–Menten curve: V_max (plateau) and K_m (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Michaelis–Menten: $v=\dfrac{V_{max}[S]}{K_m+[S]}$, with $V_{max}$ and $K_m$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times6}{2+6}=6.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\mu\text{mol L}^{-1}\text{min}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $10\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 3</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.1)^{3}}=37566.$$

**Answer.** $₹37566$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A refrigerant recovery releases $2\,\text{t}$ of CFC-11 (ODP $=1.0$) and $5\,\text{t}$ of HCFC-22 (ODP $=0.055$). The total release expressed as CFC-11-equivalent (ODP-weighted) is _____ t.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ODP-weighted release $=\sum(\text{mass}\times\text{ODP})$ — the basis on which the Montreal Protocol controls ozone-depleting substances.

**Working.**
$$2(1.0)+5(0.055)=2.275\,\text{t}.$$

**Answer.** $2.275\,\text{t CFC-11-eq}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: An aeration tank: flow $Q=10000\,\text{m}^3/\text{d}$, influent BOD $S_0=250\,\text{mg/L}$, volume $V=2000\,\text{m}^3$, MLSS $X=3000\,\text{mg/L}$. The F/M ratio is _____ d⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 350 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="40" width="110" height="70" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="115" y="78" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Aeration</text><text x="115" y="92" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">MLSS, V</text><circle cx="240" cy="75" r="34" fill="rgba(22,163,74,0.10)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="240" y="72" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Clarifier</text><path d="M20 70 l40 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="22" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Q, S0</text><path d="M170 70 l36 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M274 70 l44 0" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="290" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">Qe, Se</text><path d="M240 109 l0 36 -180 0 0 -35" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="120" y="158" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">return sludge Qr</text><path d="M250 140 l40 0" stroke="#7c2d12" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="255" y="135" font-size="8" fill="#7c2d12">waste Qw</text></svg>
figure.caption: Activated-sludge: aeration basin, secondary clarifier, sludge recycle.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dfrac{F}{M}=\dfrac{QS_0}{VX}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10000\times250}{2000\times3000}=0.417.$$

**Answer.** $0.417\,\text{d}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 150 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times150/6})\times100=95.02\%.$$

**Answer.** $95.02\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=6\%$, $O=35\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 15.8</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(6-35/8)=15786.$$

**Answer.** $15786\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">6</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$6+1.5=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $20\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.1\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">20</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.1\times20}{20}=0.1.$$

**Answer.** $0.1\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=40\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>

**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

$$\dfrac{0.3\times40}{50+40}=0.133.$$

**Answer.** $0.133$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=80\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 60</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times80}{60+80}=0.171.$$

**Answer.** $0.171$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.3\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $7$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="110" width="60" height="40" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 80</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$80e^{-0.3\times7}=9.8.$$

**Answer.** $9.8$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $6\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 25</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$6\times25=150.$$

**Answer.** $150$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.1}=6.93.$$

**Answer.** $6.93\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $6\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 5</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.06)^{5}}=37363.$$

**Answer.** $₹37363$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times200/6})\times100=98.17\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.17\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">60</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="114" width="50" height="36" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="110" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">18</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$60\times\dfrac{50}{20}+18\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=224.1.$$

**Answer.** $224.1$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=100\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 30 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 40 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times6\times40\times15}e^{-30^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00119663.$$

**Answer.** $11.966\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">4</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">1</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80+4\times28+1\times265=457.$$

**Answer.** $457$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: To hold warming below its target threshold, a remaining global carbon budget of $400\,\text{Gt CO}_2$ is available. At current emissions of $40\,\text{Gt CO}_2/\text{yr}$ (held constant), the budget is exhausted in _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Years to exhaustion $=\dfrac{\text{remaining budget}}{\text{annual emissions}}$ — the logic behind the Paris-Agreement temperature goals.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{400}{40}=10\,\text{years}.$$

**Answer.** $10$ years

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A particle ($\rho_s=2650\,\text{kg/m}^3$) settles in water ($\mu=10^{-3}\,\text{Pa·s}$). Reading its diameter from the figure, the Stokes velocity is _____ ×10⁻³ m/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="20" width="180" height="140" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.06)" stroke="#1e293b"/><circle cx="150" cy="66" r="12" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><line x1="138" y1="66" x2="162" y2="66" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/><text x="168" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">d = 0.01 mm</text><path d="M150 82 l0 52 m-4 -6 l4 6 4 -6" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="158" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">v_s</text><text x="150" y="174" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">ρ_s = 2650 kg/m³, water μ = 10⁻³ Pa·s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle diameter marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = g(ρ_s−ρ)d²/18μ, with d read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{9.81\times1650\times(0.00001)^2}{18\times10^{-3}}=0.00009.$$

**Answer.** $0.09\times10^{-3}$
